description:
Implement the InterviewProcess and InterviewSession aggregates with full CRUD APIs. Build the status transition engine as an enum state machine with role enforcement, STATUS_HISTORY writes, and 409 on invalid transitions. Publish session.status.changed events to Kafka. Implement the question bank with versioning and SESSION_QUESTION linking. Add the Redis caching layer for session lists, question sets, user roles, and client lists.

Key paths: <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/process>, <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/session>, <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/question>
functionality: create and manage interview processes and sessions, control status transitions, and link questions to sessions
benefit: the core operational workflow of the platform — scheduling and progressing candidate interviews — is fully functional

stories.summary: Implement InterviewProcess service and API endpoints
stories.description:
Implement the InterviewProcess aggregate with full CRUD. Expose endpoints: POST /processes, GET /processes, GET /processes/:id, PATCH /processes/:id. Apply Bean Validation on all request DTOs. Enforce role access: marketer and admin for writes, candidate for own-process reads.

Key paths: <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/process/InterviewProcessService.java>, <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/process/ProcessController.java>

acceptanceCriteria.given: an authenticated marketer sends a valid POST /processes with a client ID, candidate ID, and technology
acceptanceCriteria.then: a new InterviewProcess is persisted with status CREATED and a 201 response is returned

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Candidate can only see their own processes
acceptanceCriteria.then: only processes where the candidate ID matches the authenticated user are returned

acceptanceCriteria.given: a POST /processes request is sent with a missing client ID
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 400 Bad Request with field-level errors is returned

tasks.summary: Implement InterviewProcessEntity, repository, and service
tasks.description: Create JPA entity mapping to interview_processes table. Add ProcessRepository. Implement ProcessService with create, read, update methods and @PreAuthorize role guards.

tasks.summary: Implement ProcessController with request/response DTOs
tasks.description: Create REST controller with all CRUD endpoints. Add Jakarta Bean Validation annotations on CreateProcessRequest and UpdateProcessRequest DTOs.

tasks.summary: Add integration tests for process endpoints
tasks.description: Use Spring Boot Test + Testcontainers Postgres. Cover CRUD operations and role enforcement for marketer, admin, and candidate.

stories.summary: Implement InterviewSession service and API endpoints
stories.description:
Implement InterviewSession as a child aggregate of InterviewProcess. Expose endpoints: POST /processes/:id/sessions, GET /processes/:id/sessions, GET /sessions/:id, PATCH /sessions/:id. Session creation is restricted to marketer and admin. Candidates may read sessions belonging to their own processes only.

Key paths: <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/session/InterviewSessionService.java>, <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/session/SessionController.java>

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Marketer schedules a session under a process
acceptanceCriteria.given: an authenticated marketer sends a valid POST /processes/:id/sessions with scheduled_at and round_number
acceptanceCriteria.then: a session is persisted with status SCHEDULED and a 201 response is returned

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Candidate cannot access sessions from another candidate's process
acceptanceCriteria.given: an authenticated candidate calls GET /sessions/:id for a session in a different candidate's process

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Session list for a process is returned correctly
acceptanceCriteria.given: a process has 3 sessions across different rounds
acceptanceCriteria.when: GET /processes/:id/sessions is called by an admin
acceptanceCriteria.then: all 3 sessions are returned ordered by round_number

tasks.summary: Implement InterviewSessionEntity, repository, and service
tasks.description: Create JPA entity with FK to interview_processes. Implement SessionService with CRUD and role-scoped query methods.

tasks.summary: Implement SessionController with nested route under processes
tasks.description: Expose session endpoints with proper parent-process ownership validation. Add Bean Validation on CreateSessionRequest.

tasks.summary: Add integration tests for session endpoints
tasks.description: Cover: session creation under a process, candidate access scoping, non-existent process returns 404, role enforcement.

stories.summary: Build status transition engine with role enforcement and STATUS_HISTORY audit trail
stories.description:
Implement SessionStatusTransitionService as an enum state machine defining all valid status transitions and which roles may trigger each. On every transition, write a record to STATUS_HISTORY including actor ID, from_status, to_status, changed_at, and change_source. Return 409 Conflict on invalid transitions. Expose PATCH /sessions/:id/status for manual transitions.

Valid transitions include: SCHEDULED→IN_REVIEW (background job or supporter), IN_REVIEW→PENDING_FEEDBACK (supporter), PENDING_FEEDBACK→FEEDBACK_SUBMITTED (supporter), FEEDBACK_SUBMITTED→OUTCOME_SET (supporter), and marketer-owned pre-interview transitions.

Key paths: <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/session/SessionStatusTransitionService.java>, <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/session/SessionStatus.java>

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Valid transition succeeds and writes STATUS_HISTORY
acceptanceCriteria.given: a session is in SCHEDULED status and a supporter calls PATCH /sessions/:id/status with IN_REVIEW
acceptanceCriteria.when: the transition service processes the request
acceptanceCriteria.then: the session status is updated to IN_REVIEW and a STATUS_HISTORY row is written with actor ID, from_status=SCHEDULED, to_status=IN_REVIEW

acceptanceCriteria.given: a session is in FEEDBACK_SUBMITTED status
acceptanceCriteria.when: a PATCH /sessions/:id/status request attempts to transition to SCHEDULED
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 409 Conflict response is returned and no STATUS_HISTORY row is written

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Role not permitted for transition returns 403
acceptanceCriteria.given: a session is in IN_REVIEW status
acceptanceCriteria.when: a marketer attempts to transition it to PENDING_FEEDBACK

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Background job transition is recorded with correct change_source
acceptanceCriteria.given: the background job transitions a session from SCHEDULED to IN_REVIEW
acceptanceCriteria.then: the STATUS_HISTORY row has change_source=BACKGROUND_JOB and actor is the system user

tasks.summary: Define SessionStatus enum and valid transition map
tasks.description: Create SessionStatus enum with all states. Build an EnumMap defining permitted (from → to) pairs and the set of roles allowed per transition.

tasks.summary: Implement SessionStatusTransitionService
tasks.description: Validate the requested transition against the state machine. Throw InvalidTransitionException (→ 409) on invalid pairs. Throw AccessDeniedException (→ 403) on role mismatch.

tasks.summary: Implement STATUS_HISTORY writer
tasks.description: After each successful transition, persist a StatusHistory record with actor_id, from_status, to_status, changed_at (UTC), and change_source.

tasks.summary: Expose PATCH /sessions/:id/status endpoint
tasks.description: Wire the transition service into a controller endpoint. Accept target status in the request body. Return 200 with updated session on success.

tasks.summary: Add unit and integration tests for transition engine
tasks.description: Unit test all valid and invalid transition pairs. Integration test: valid transition persists STATUS_HISTORY, invalid returns 409, wrong role returns 403.

stories.summary: Implement Kafka producer for session.status.changed events
stories.description:
On every successful session status transition, publish a domain event to the Kafka topic session.status.changed. The event payload must include session ID, process ID, from_status, to_status, changed_at, and change_source. Configure the Kafka producer with idempotent delivery and appropriate serialisation.

Key paths: <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/session/SessionStatusEventPublisher.java>

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Status change publishes event to Kafka
acceptanceCriteria.given: a session transitions from SCHEDULED to IN_REVIEW
acceptanceCriteria.then: a message is published to the session.status.changed topic containing session ID, process ID, from_status, to_status, and changed_at

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Failed transition does not publish event
acceptanceCriteria.given: a transition attempt is rejected with 409
acceptanceCriteria.then: no message is published to Kafka

tasks.summary: Configure Kafka producer with idempotent delivery
tasks.description: Add spring-kafka dependency. Configure KafkaTemplate with enable.idempotence=true, acks=all, and JSON value serialiser. Read broker addresses from environment.

tasks.summary: Implement SessionStatusEventPublisher
tasks.description: Create a Spring component that publishes a SessionStatusChangedEvent DTO to session.status.changed after each successful transition. Called from SessionStatusTransitionService.

tasks.summary: Add integration test with Testcontainers Kafka
tasks.description: Use Testcontainers Kafka to verify that a status transition results in a message being consumable from the topic with the correct payload.

stories.summary: Implement Question Bank CRUD with versioning and SESSION_QUESTION linking
stories.description:
Implement the question bank with create, read, update, and soft-delete endpoints. Each update creates a new version row in question_versions preserving the prior text. Implement SESSION_QUESTION join table management: POST /sessions/:id/questions and DELETE /sessions/:id/questions/:qid to link and unlink questions. Admin can perform all write operations; supporter can link/unlink questions on their assigned sessions.

Key paths: <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/question>

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Updating a question creates a version record
acceptanceCriteria.given: an admin sends PATCH /questions/:id with updated question text
acceptanceCriteria.then: the question row is updated and a new row is written to question_versions with the previous text and version number

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Supporter can link a question to their session
acceptanceCriteria.given: an authenticated supporter is assigned to a session
acceptanceCriteria.when: POST /sessions/:id/questions is called with a valid question ID
acceptanceCriteria.then: a SESSION_QUESTION row is created and a 201 response is returned

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Supporter cannot link questions to another supporter's session
acceptanceCriteria.given: a supporter who is not assigned to a session

tasks.summary: Implement QuestionEntity, versioning, and repository
tasks.description: Create JPA entity for questions table. Implement version-on-update logic in QuestionService: before update, copy current values to question_versions.

tasks.summary: Implement Question CRUD controller and DTOs
tasks.description: Expose POST /questions, GET /questions/:id, PATCH /questions/:id, DELETE /questions/:id (soft delete). Add Bean Validation on DTOs. Admin-only for write operations.

tasks.summary: Implement SESSION_QUESTION link and unlink endpoints
tasks.description: Create SessionQuestionService and expose POST /sessions/:id/questions and DELETE /sessions/:id/questions/:qid. Validate session assignment ownership for supporters.

tasks.summary: Add integration tests for question bank and session linking
tasks.description: Cover: create question, update creates version, link question to session, unlink, role enforcement, and linking to non-owned session returns 403.

stories.summary: Implement Redis caching layer for session lists, question sets, user roles, and client lists
stories.description:
Add Spring Cache with Redis as the cache provider. Apply caching to high-read entities: session list per process (5min TTL), question set per session (10min TTL), user role by user ID (15min TTL), and end client list (30min TTL). Configure cache eviction on relevant write operations.

Cached operations and TTLs:
- session list per process: 5 min, evict on session create/update
- question set per session: 10 min, evict on SESSION_QUESTION change
- user role by user ID: 15 min, evict on user role update
- end client list: 30 min, evict on client create/update

Key paths: <apps/api/src/main/java/com/ims/config/CacheConfig.java>

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Session list is served from cache on repeated reads
acceptanceCriteria.when: the same endpoint is called a second time within 5 minutes
acceptanceCriteria.then: the database is not queried on the second call and the response is returned from Redis

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Cache is evicted on session update
acceptanceCriteria.given: a cached session list exists for a process
acceptanceCriteria.when: a session under that process is updated
acceptanceCriteria.then: the cache entry is evicted and the next read hits the database

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Redis unavailability does not crash the application
acceptanceCriteria.then: the request falls through to the database and returns a correct response

tasks.summary: Configure Spring Cache with Redis and RedisCacheManager
tasks.description: Add spring-boot-starter-data-redis and spring-boot-starter-cache. Configure RedisCacheManager with per-cache TTL settings. Read Redis host from environment.

tasks.summary: Apply @Cacheable and @CacheEvict annotations to service methods
tasks.description: Annotate session list, question set, user role, and client list read methods with @Cacheable. Add @CacheEvict on corresponding write methods.

tasks.summary: Add integration tests for cache behaviour with Testcontainers Redis
tasks.description: Verify cache hit (no DB call on second read) and cache eviction (DB called after write) using Testcontainers Redis.
